heroHeadline: New DHS Secretary Pushes Quieter Enforcement Amid MAGA Backlash; El Gamal Family Re-arrested Despite Court Order; Deportation Flight Halted; House Ends 71-Day DHS Shutdown; Appeals Court Blocks Asylum Ban
heroSubtitle: ICE re-arrested Egyptian mother Hayam El Gamal and her five children at the Denver ICE check-in office on April 26 — less than 48 hours after a federal judge ordered their release — and placed them on a deportation flight before multiple emergency courts halted the aircraft mid-air, forcing it to return to Denver and securing the family's release after 320+ days in ICE custody. On the same day, the House passed H.R. 7147 extending DHS funding through May 22, ending the 71-day partial shutdown without any ICE accountability mandates demanded by Democrats after the January Minneapolis killings. On April 24, a federal appeals court ruled Trump's executive order suspending asylum at the southern border violates the Immigration and Nationality Act — one of the most significant judicial limits on presidential immigration power in years, with the administration expected to appeal to the Supreme Court. NPR reported 2,145 ICE detainees are now held over one year — up 115% since October 2025. DOJ internal quotas directing 100–200 denaturalization cases per month, vs. a historical average of 11 per year, were publicly documented. FY2026 ICE custody deaths remain at 29, a 22-year record.
footerNote: Data sourced from DHS, ICE official records, ACLU, HRW, DHS OIG, TRAC Reports, Detention Watch Network, court records, and Congressional records
